name: Panama
avoid: May to November is wetter across both coasts, and September to October is the least reliable window for San Blas boat rides, jungle hikes, and Bocas snorkeling.
Christmas, New Year, Carnival week, and Semana Santa push up Air Panama fares, Casco Viejo hotel prices, and island lodge availability very quickly.
San Blas is cash-heavy, rustic, and weather dependent, so do not plan tight same-day international connections around the Guna Yala 4x4 and boat transfers.
stopoverNote: Panama City is the easiest Copa stopover hub in the country, and it is also where Miraflores visits, Albrook domestic flights, and San Blas 4x4 departures connect most smoothly.

cities.name: Panama City
cities.notes: Modern high-rise capital for the canal, Frank Gehry's Biomuseo, seafood markets, and easy access to Amador Causeway and Albrook transport links.

cities.name: Casco Viejo
cities.notes: Historic quarter of Panamá City for churches, plazas, canal history, rooftop dining, and the easiest old-town base before or after island days.

cities.name: San Blas Islands
cities.notes: Guna Yala archipelago of white-sand cays, simple island cabins, boat transfers from Cartí, and the most castaway-style beach stop in Panama.

cities.name: Boquete
cities.notes: Cooler Chiriquí highlands base for coffee farms, cloud-forest trails, waterfalls, and a slower inland break after the coast.

cities.name: Bocas del Toro
cities.notes: Caribbean island chain anchored by Bocas Town for water taxis, Red Frog Beach, Starfish Beach, and easy reef-and-beach day tours.

itinerary.sim: +Móvil or Tigo prepaid/eSIM plans are the easiest pickup in Panama City; expect roughly ₹840–₹1,260 for 10–15GB. Download offline maps before San Blas and for long Bocas water-taxi days because coverage drops quickly once you leave the city or main islands.

Panama City.name: Panama City
Panama City.note: Stay around El Cangrejo, Obarrio, or Punta Paitilla if you want easier road access to Miraflores and Albrook, then shift to Casco only if you specifically want old-town evenings.

activities.name: Miraflores Visitor Center and IMAX at the Panama Canal
Amador Causeway walk or bike rental with Panama City skyline views
Panama sign and Flamenco Marina promenade
Seafood stop at Mercado de Mariscos

hotels.name: Best Western Plus Panama Zen Hotel

activities.name: Biomuseo admission
Metropolitan Natural Park trails and lookout
Cinta Costera waterfront walk
Albrook Mall or Albrook bus terminal recon for onward travel

hotels.name: Hotel El Panama by Faranda Grand

Casco Viejo.name: Casco Viejo
Casco Viejo.note: Casco is compact and walkable by day, but its cobbled lanes get busy at night, so use rideshares for late returns and reserve rooftop dinners ahead on weekends.

activities.name: Museo del Canal Interoceánico de Panamá
Plaza de la Independencia, Cathedral Basilica, and Iglesia de San José
Paseo de las Bóvedas and Plaza de Francia
Sunset drinks with bay views from a Casco rooftop
Panamá Viejo archaeological site and museum
Arco Chato and Casa Góngora cultural stop
Casco lanes for mola shopping, coffee, and photo stops
Rooftop or courtyard dinner back in Casco Viejo

San Blas Islands.name: San Blas Islands
San Blas Islands.note: Bring passport, dry bags, and enough cash in small USD notes; most islands have limited power, no meaningful ATMs, and meals are usually fixed by your lodge.

activities.name: Shared 4x4 from Panama City to Cartí port
Guna Yala entry tax and checkpoint fees
Lancha transfer to your island lodge
Snorkeling around Isla Perro Chico's sunken-boat reef
Boat trip covering Natural Pool, Chichime, and a second snorkel stop
Kayak or paddleboard rental if your island offers it
Beach time on a small Guna-run cay with hammocks and coconut palms
Fresh lobster dinner in season

Boquete.name: Boquete
Boquete.note: Boquete is cooler than the coasts, so keep one light layer for early hikes and pre-book coffee tours or private transfers if you arrive from David late in the day.

activities.name: Pipeline Trail (Sendero Cascada Escondida)
Mi Jardín es Su Jardín
Boquete town center, flowers, and river walk
Boquete Brewing Company taproom stop
The Lost Waterfalls hike
Finca Lérida coffee tour and tasting
Boquete lookout drive or taxi toward Bajo Mono
Local produce and coffee shopping in central Boquete

Bocas del Toro.name: Bocas del Toro
Bocas del Toro.note: Base yourself in Bocas Town if you want the simplest water-taxi access; carry cash for beaches and boats, and plan around afternoon rain or choppier seas in the wetter months.

activities.name: Water taxi round trip from Bocas Town to Red Frog Beach
Red Frog Beach entry
Bocas Town waterfront and Simón Bolívar Park
Sunset drink or dinner on the Bocas overwater strip

days.theme: Dolphin Bay, Zapatilla or Starfish Beach Day

activities.name: Shared boat tour through Dolphin Bay with reef or mangrove stops
Boca del Drago and Starfish Beach transport
Optional snorkeling gear rental
Evening water-taxi hop to Isla Carenero for dinner

connections.method: Uber, taxi, or metro plus a short cab ride in about 20–30 minutes, depending on traffic
Pre-booked shared 4x4 from your hotel to Cartí plus lancha transfer to the island; public transport is impractical for this route
Return by boat and 4x4 to Panama City, then continue from Albrook bus terminal on the overnight coach to David plus a local bus or taxi up to Boquete
Shared shuttle or local bus to Almirante, then water taxi to Isla Colón; fastest day-flight alternatives are limited and seasonal

extras: Dry season from December to April is the cleanest fit for a one-trip loop that combines Miraflores, San Blas boats, Boquete hikes, and Bocas beaches.
Use cash for San Blas taxes, many water taxis, and smaller island or highland purchases even though cards work well in Panama City and some Bocas hotels.
Air Panama flights from PAC at Albrook save a lot of time if you need to shortcut the westbound overland legs, especially on the Bocas return.
If you only have a week, keep Panama City, Casco Viejo, and either San Blas or Bocas as the core and save Boquete for a longer second trip.
Guna Yala lodges are rustic by design, so bring a power bank, dry bag, reef-safe sun protection, and low expectations on Wi‑Fi or hot water.

Casco Viejo: Plaza de Francia Casco Viejo Panama
Bocas del Toro: Red Frog Beach Bocas del Toro
